title: ophrys cornuta var. phrygia
intro: Ophrys cornuta var. phrygia, also known as the Phrygian bee orchid, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Ophrys cornuta plant, a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which are believed to help soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as treat infections. The health benefits of Ophrys cornuta var. phrygia include its potential to aid in the healing of wounds, reduce inflammation, and protect against fungal infections. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, and its medicinal properties can be utilized through various herbal preparations, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
